sleep.title: Improve Sleep Quality
messages: You're getting less than 6 hours of sleep. Aim for 7–9 hours to reduce stress and improve cognitive function.
Maintain a consistent sleep schedule — even on weekends — to regulate your body clock.
Create a relaxing bedtime routine: dim lights 1 hour before bed and avoid stimulating content.
Keep your bedroom cool (65–68°F / 18–20°C) for optimal sleep quality.

digital_detox.title: Digital Detox
messages: Your daily screen time is high. Try to limit recreational screen use to under 4 hours per day.
Use app timers or digital wellbeing tools to set daily limits on social media and entertainment apps.
Take a 5-minute screen break every hour to reduce eye strain and mental fatigue.
Designate 'screen-free' times — such as during meals and the first 30 minutes after waking.

social_media.title: Social Media Balance
messages: Excessive social media use is linked to elevated stress and anxiety. Try limiting to 1–2 hours per day.
Unfollow or mute accounts that cause negative emotions or comparison.
Batch your social media checks: twice a day rather than constant checking.
Consider a 7-day social media detox to reset your baseline stress levels.

gaming.title: Gaming Balance
messages: High gaming hours, especially late at night, can disrupt sleep and elevate cortisol.
Set a hard stop time for gaming — at least 1 hour before you plan to sleep.
Balance gaming with physical activity: for every hour of gaming, do 15 minutes of movement.
Avoid competitive games before bed as they stimulate adrenaline and make it harder to fall asleep.

movement.title: Increase Physical Activity
messages: You're under the recommended 8,000 steps per day. Try a 20-minute walk during lunch.
Physical activity is one of the most effective natural stress reducers. Even light exercise helps.
Try the 'exercise snack' approach: three 10-minute walks spread across the day.
Strength training 2–3 times per week has been shown to significantly lower chronic stress markers.

mindfulness.title: Mindfulness & Relaxation
messages: High mental fatigue detected. Try 5–10 minutes of deep breathing or meditation daily.
Box breathing (4 counts in, hold, out, hold) activates the parasympathetic nervous system.
Progressive muscle relaxation before bed can reduce tension and improve sleep onset.
Journaling for 5 minutes daily can help process stress and reduce rumination.

caffeine.title: Caffeine Management
messages: High caffeine intake can elevate cortisol and anxiety. Try to stay under 400mg per day (~4 cups of coffee).
Avoid caffeine after 2 PM to prevent sleep disruption.
Replace afternoon coffee with herbal tea or water for better sleep quality.
Gradually taper caffeine intake to avoid withdrawal headaches if reducing consumption.

high_stress.title: High Stress Action Plan
messages: Your stress score is high. Consider reaching out to a mental health professional or counselor.
Identify your top 3 stressors and make an action plan to address or reduce at least one.
Practice the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique when feeling overwhelmed.
Prioritize rest, nourishment, and connection with trusted friends or family this week.

positive.title: Keep It Up!
messages: Your behavioral metrics look healthy. You're managing sleep, activity, and screen time well.
Great job maintaining physical activity! Keep up the consistent movement routine.
Your sleep duration and quality look solid — this is one of the best defenses against stress.
Continue your current habits and consider tracking your mood to identify subtle stress patterns.
